title: Constant parameters in a PreparedStatement should not be set more than once
description: When using JDBC's PreparedStatement, setting the same constant parameter multiple times is redundant and can lead to confusion or errors. Each constant parameter should be set only once to ensure clarity and maintainability of the code.
recommendation: Set each constant parameter in a PreparedStatement only once. If a parameter value does not change, avoid resetting it multiple times.
impact: Redundant parameter settings can make code harder to read and maintain, and may introduce subtle bugs if the value is accidentally changed.

checks.message: Set each constant parameter in a PreparedStatement only once. If a parameter value does not change, avoid resetting it multiple times.
